postal — Pub/Sub message bus for JavaScript and TypeScript. Wildcard subscriptions, channel-scoped messaging, zero dependencies.

Repo: https://github.com/postaljs/postal.js
Docs: https://postal-js.org
License: MIT
Version: 3.0.0

---

PACKAGES

postal (npm: "postal")
  Core library. Channels, pub/sub, RPC, wiretaps, transport integration.
  packages/postal/

postal-transport-messageport (npm: "postal-transport-messageport")
  Bridges postal across iframes, web workers, and Node.js worker threads via MessagePort + handshake.
  packages/postal-transport-messageport/
  Two entry points:
    "postal-transport-messageport"      — browser APIs (iframes, web workers)
    "postal-transport-messageport/node" — Node.js worker_threads helpers + shared core

postal-transport-broadcastchannel (npm: "postal-transport-broadcastchannel")
  Bridges postal across same-origin tabs/windows via BroadcastChannel.
  packages/postal-transport-broadcastchannel/

postal-transport-uds (npm: "postal-transport-uds")
  Bridges postal across independent Node.js processes via Unix domain sockets + NDJSON.
  packages/postal-transport-uds/
  Hub-and-spoke topology: one server, N clients. No parent/child relationship required.

@postal/docs (private)
  Starlight documentation site. Not published to npm.
  packages/docs/

---

CORE API — package: postal

Functions:
  getChannel(name)           → Channel<TMap>   Singleton registry lookup/create
  addWiretap(callback)       → () => void       Global observer, sees all envelopes
  resetWiretaps()            → void             Clear all wiretaps
  addTransport(transport, options?) → () => void  Register a transport bridge
  resetTransports()          → void             Remove all transports
  resetChannels()            → void             Tear down all channels + transports (test isolation)

Channel<TMap> methods:
  .name                      readonly string
  .subscribe(pattern, callback) → () => void    AMQP wildcard pattern matching
  .publish(topic, payload)   → void             Fan-out to matching subscribers
  .request(topic, payload, options?) → Promise  RPC — correlates to a handle() responder
  .handle(topic, callback)   → () => void       RPC responder — one per topic per channel
  .dispose()                 → void             Tear down channel, reject pending RPCs

Types:
  Channel<TMap>              Channel instance type
  ChannelRegistry            Augmentable interface for typed channel maps
  Envelope<TPayload>         { id, type, channel, topic, payload, timestamp, source?, replyTo?, correlationId? }
  EnvelopeType               "publish" | "request" | "reply"
  Transport                  { send, subscribe, dispose? }
  TransportFilter            { channels?, topics? }
  TransportOptions           { filter? }
  RequestOptions             { timeout? }  (default: 5000ms)
  SubscriberCallback<T>      (envelope: Envelope<T>) => void

Error classes:
  PostalTimeoutError         request() timed out. Props: channel, topic, timeout
  PostalRpcError             handler threw, error relayed to requester. Props: code?
  PostalDisposedError        method called on disposed channel. Props: channel

---

CHANNEL TYPE SYSTEM

Two ways to type a channel:

1. Explicit type map — pass TMap to getChannel:

  type OrderMap = { 'item.placed': { sku: string; qty: number } };
  const orders = getChannel<OrderMap>('orders');
  // publish / subscribe / request / handle are all typed

2. ChannelRegistry augmentation — declare once, infer everywhere:

  declare module 'postal' {
    interface ChannelRegistry {
      orders: {
        'item.placed': { sku: string; qty: number };
        'item.cancelled': { sku: string; reason: string };
      };
    }
  }

  const orders = getChannel('orders'); // OrderMap inferred from registry

RPC topic shape — use { request, response } to mark a topic as RPC-only:

  declare module 'postal' {
    interface ChannelRegistry {
      pricing: {
        'quote.calculate': { request: { sku: string }; response: { price: number } };
      };
    }
  }

  // publish() rejects RPC topics at compile time; use request()/handle() instead

PayloadFor<TMap, Pattern> — type utility resolving payload union for wildcard patterns:
  'order.*' → union of all single-segment children
  'order.#' → order itself + all descendants
  '#'        → union of all values
  '*'        → single-segment keys only

---

TRANSPORT SYSTEM

Transports implement: { send(envelope), subscribe(callback) => unsubscribe, dispose?() }

Echo prevention: outbound envelopes are stamped with instanceId as `source`.
Inbound handler drops envelopes where source === local instanceId.
Filtering: TransportFilter restricts which channels/topics a transport forwards.
Reply envelopes always bypass filters (to complete RPC round-trips).

addTransport() wires the outbound hook and inbound listener.
resetChannels() automatically calls resetTransports() via internal onReset hook.

---

MESSAGEPORT TRANSPORT — package: postal-transport-messageport

Default entry point exports (browser):
  createMessagePortTransport(port)           Low-level: wraps a connected MessagePort
  connectToIframe(iframe, options?)          Parent side of iframe handshake → Promise<Transport>
  connectToParent(options?)                  Iframe side of iframe handshake → Promise<Transport>
  connectToWorker(worker, options?)          Main thread side of worker handshake → Promise<Transport>
  connectToHost(options?)                    Worker side of worker handshake → Promise<Transport>
  markTransferable(payload, transferables)   Mark payload for zero-copy transfer
  PostalHandshakeTimeoutError                Handshake timed out
  ConnectOptions                             { timeout?, targetOrigin?, allowedOrigin? }
  PROTOCOL_VERSION                           Number (currently 1)

Node.js entry point exports (postal-transport-messageport/node):
  connectToWorkerThread(worker, options?)    Main thread side of worker_threads handshake → Promise<Transport>
  connectFromWorkerThread(options?)          Worker thread side of handshake → Promise<Transport>
  createMessagePortTransport(port)           Same as default entry point
  markTransferable(payload, transferables)   Same as default entry point
  PostalHandshakeTimeoutError                Same as default entry point
  PROTOCOL_VERSION                           Same as default entry point

Handshake sequence:
  1. Initiator creates MessageChannel, sends { type:"postal:syn" } via postMessage, transfers port2
  2. Receiver gets port2, sends { type:"postal:ack" } through it
  3. Initiator receives ack on port1, both sides wrap ports in createMessagePortTransport()

Node.js worker_threads use the same handshake, but connectToWorkerThread sends through
worker.postMessage() and connectFromWorkerThread listens on parentPort.

---

UDS TRANSPORT — package: postal-transport-uds

Exports:
  listenOnSocket(socketPath, options?)          Server: listen, accept N clients → Promise<{ dispose }>
  connectToSocket(socketPath, options?)         Client: connect, handshake → Promise<() => void>
  createSocketTransport(socket, serializer?)    Low-level: wraps a net.Socket in a Transport
  PostalUdsHandshakeTimeoutError                Handshake timed out
  PROTOCOL_VERSION                              Number (currently 1)
  UdsServerOptions                              { filter?, unlinkStale?, timeout? }
  UdsConnectOptions                             { timeout?, onDisconnect? }
  UdsSocket                                     Minimal socket interface type
  Serializer                                    { encode, decode } — pluggable wire format

Handshake: client sends postal:uds-syn, server responds postal:uds-ack over NDJSON.
Wire format: NDJSON (JSON.stringify + "\n"). Serializer interface allows swapping to MessagePack.
Topology: hub-and-spoke. Server registers each client as a separate transport via addTransport().
Fan-out and echo prevention handled by postal core, not the server.
No auto-reconnection. onDisconnect callback is the seam for consumer retry logic.
Server unlinks stale socket files before listen() by default (unlinkStale: true).
Node.js only (node:net, node:fs). Requires Node >= 22.

---

BROADCASTCHANNEL TRANSPORT — package: postal-transport-broadcastchannel

Exports:
  createBroadcastChannelTransport(name?)     name defaults to "postal"

No handshake. Same-origin only (browser BroadcastChannel restriction).
Echo prevention is handled by postal core (source field on Envelope).
Multiple tabs calling createBroadcastChannelTransport("postal") form a mesh automatically.

---

WILDCARD MATCHING

Topics: dot-delimited strings ("order.placed", "user.profile.updated")
*  — matches exactly one segment: "order.*" matches "order.placed", not "order.placed.rush"
#  — matches zero or more segments: "order.#" matches "order", "order.placed", "order.placed.rush"

Results are cached in a module-level Map for repeated lookups.

---

BUILD / TEST / LINT

pnpm install                                          Install all dependencies
pnpm build                                            Build all packages
pnpm test                                             Run all tests (turbo -> jest)
pnpm lint                                             Lint all packages
pnpm run checks                                       lint + test + build (CI gate)

pnpm --filter postal test                             Core library tests only
pnpm --filter postal-transport-messageport test       MessagePort transport tests
pnpm --filter postal-transport-broadcastchannel test  BroadcastChannel transport tests
pnpm --filter postal-transport-uds test               UDS transport tests
pnpm --filter postal test -- --watch                  Watch mode

Toolchain: tsdown (CJS + ESM + .d.ts), Jest + ts-jest, ESLint 9, TypeScript 5.9+, strict mode.
Tests live alongside source in *.test.ts files.
